verses.latin_vulgate: Et cum evasissemus, tunc cognovimus quia Melita insula vocabatur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ διασωθέντες τότε ἐπέγνωσαν ὅτι μελίτη ἡ νῆσος καλεῖται
verses.king_james: And when they were escaped, then they knew that the island was called Melita.

verses.latin_vulgate: Barbari vero præstabant non modicam humanitatem nobis. Accensa enim pyra, reficiebant nos omnes propter imbrem qui imminebat, et frigus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἵ δέ βάρβαροι παρεῖχον οὐ τὴν τυχοῦσαν φιλανθρωπίαν ἡμῖν ἀνάψαντες γὰρ πυρὰν προσελάβοντο πάντας ἡμᾶς διὰ τὸν ὑετὸν τὸν ἐφεστῶτα καὶ διὰ τὸ ψῦχος
verses.king_james: And the barbarous people shewed us no little kindness: for they kindled a fire, and received us every one, because of the present rain, and because of the cold.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum congregasset autem Paulus sarmentorum aliquantam multitudinem, et imposuisset super ignem, vipera a calore cum processisset, invasit manum ejus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: συστρέψαντος δὲ τοῦ παύλου φρυγάνων πλῆθος καὶ ἐπιθέντος ἐπὶ τὴν πυράν ἔχιδνα ἐκ τῆς θέρμης ἐξελθοῦσα καθῆψεν τῆς χειρὸς αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: And when Paul had gathered a bundle of sticks, and laid *them* on the fire, there came a viper out of the heat, and fastened on his hand.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ut vero viderunt barbari pendentem bestiam de manu ejus, ad invicem dicebant: Utique homicida est homo hic, qui cum evaserit de mari, ultio non sinit eum vivere.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὡς δὲ εἶδον οἱ βάρβαροι κρεμάμενον τὸ θηρίον ἐκ τῆς χειρὸς αὐτοῦ ἔλεγον πρὸς ἀλλήλους πάντως φονεύς ἐστιν ὁ ἄνθρωπος οὗτος ὃν διασωθέντα ἐκ τῆς θαλάσσης ἡ δίκη ζῆν οὐκ εἴασεν
verses.king_james: And when the barbarians saw the *venomous* beast hang on his hand, they said among themselves, No doubt this man is a murderer, whom, though he hath escaped the sea, yet vengeance suffereth not to live.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ille quidem excutiens bestiam in ignem, nihil mali passus est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ μὲν οὖν ἀποτινάξας τὸ θηρίον εἰς τὸ πῦρ ἔπαθεν οὐδὲν κακόν
verses.king_james: And he shook off the beast into the fire, and felt no harm.

verses.latin_vulgate: At illi existimabant eum in tumorem convertendum, et subito casurum et mori. Diu autem illis exspectantibus, et videntibus nihil mali in eo fieri, convertentes se, dicebant eum esse deum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἱ δὲ προσεδόκων αὐτὸν μέλλειν πίμπρασθαι ἢ καταπίπτειν ἄφνω νεκρόν ἐπὶ πολὺ δὲ αὐτῶν προσδοκώντων καὶ θεωρούντων μηδὲν ἄτοπον εἰς αὐτὸν γινόμενον μεταβαλλόμενοι ἔλεγον θεόν αὐτὸν εἶναι
verses.king_james: Howbeit they looked when he should have swollen, or fallen down dead suddenly: but after they had looked a great while, and saw no harm come to him, they changed their minds, and said that he was a god.

verses.latin_vulgate: In locis autem illis erant prædia principis insulæ, nomine Publii, qui nos suscipiens, triduo benigne exhibuit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐν δὲ τοῖς περὶ τὸν τόπον ἐκεῖνον ὑπῆρχεν χωρία τῷ πρώτῳ τῆς νήσου ὀνόματι ποπλίῳ ὃς ἀναδεξάμενος ἡμᾶς τρεῖς ἡμέρας φιλοφρόνως ἐξένισεν
verses.king_james: In the same quarters were possessions of the chief man of the island, whose name was Publius; who received us, and lodged us three days courteously.

verses.latin_vulgate: Contigit autem patrem Publii febribus et dysenteria vexatum jacere. Ad quem Paulus intravit: et cum orasset, et imposuisset ei manus, salvavit eum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐγένετο δὲ τὸν πατέρα τοῦ ποπλίου πυρετοῖς καὶ δυσεντερίᾳ συνεχόμενον κατακεῖσθαι πρὸς ὃν ὁ παῦλος εἰσελθὼν καὶ προσευξάμενος ἐπιθεὶς τὰς χεῖρας αὐτῷ ἰάσατο αὐτόν
verses.king_james: And it came to pass, that the father of Publius lay sick of a fever and of a bloody flux: to whom Paul entered in, and prayed, and laid his hands on him, and healed him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quo facto, omnes qui in insula habebant infirmitates, accedebant, et curabantur:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τούτου οὖν γενομένου καὶ οἱ λοιποὶ οἱ ἔχοντες ἀσθενείας ἐν τῇ νήσῳ προσήρχοντο καὶ ἐθεραπεύοντο
verses.king_james: So when this was done, others also, which had diseases in the island, came, and were healed:

verses.latin_vulgate: qui etiam multis honoribus nos honoraverunt, et navigantibus imposuerunt quæ necessaria erant.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἳ καὶ πολλαῖς τιμαῖς ἐτίμησαν ἡμᾶς καὶ ἀναγομένοις ἐπέθεντο τὰ πρὸς τὴν χρείαν
verses.king_james: Who also honoured us with many honours; and when we departed, they laded *us* with such things as were necessary.

verses.latin_vulgate: Post menses autem tres navigavimus in navi Alexandrina, quæ in insula hiemaverat, cui erat insigne Castorum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μετὰ δὲ τρεῖς μῆνας ἀνήχθημεν ἐν πλοίῳ παρακεχειμακότι ἐν τῇ νήσῳ ἀλεξανδρίνῳ παρασήμῳ διοσκούροις
verses.king_james: And after three months we departed in a ship of Alexandria, which had wintered in the isle, whose sign was Castor and Pollux.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et cum venissemus Syracusam, mansimus ibi triduo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ καταχθέντες εἰς συρακούσας ἐπεμείναμεν ἡμέρας τρεῖς
verses.king_james: And landing at Syracuse, we tarried *there* three days.

verses.latin_vulgate: Inde circumlegentes devenimus Rhegium: et post unum diem, flante austro, secunda die venimus Puteolos:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅθεν περιελθόντες κατηντήσαμεν εἰς ῥήγιον καὶ μετὰ μίαν ἡμέραν ἐπιγενομένου νότου δευτεραῖοι ἤλθομεν εἰς ποτιόλους
verses.king_james: And from thence we fetched a compass, and came to Rhegium: and after one day the south wind blew, and we came the next day to Puteoli:

verses.latin_vulgate: ubi inventis fratribus rogati sumus manere apud eos dies septem: et sic venimus Romam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὗ εὑρόντες ἀδελφοὺς παρεκλήθημεν ἐπ᾽ αὐτοῖς ἐπιμεῖναι ἡμέρας ἑπτά καὶ οὕτως εἰς τὴν ῥώμην ἤλθομεν
verses.king_james: Where we found brethren, and were desired to tarry with them seven days: and so we went toward Rome.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et inde cum audissent fratres, occurrerunt nobis usque ad Apii forum, ac tres Tabernas. Quos cum vidisset Paulus, gratias agens Deo, accepit fiduciam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: κἀκεῖθεν οἱ ἀδελφοὶ ἀκούσαντες τὰ περὶ ἡμῶν ἐξῆλθον εἰς ἀπάντησιν ἡμῖν ἄχρις ἀππίου φόρου καὶ τριῶν ταβερνῶν οὓς ἰδὼν ὁ παῦλος εὐχαριστήσας τῷ θεῷ ἔλαβεν θάρσος
verses.king_james: And from thence, when the brethren heard of us, they came to meet us as far as Appii forum, and The three taverns: whom when Paul saw, he thanked God, and took courage.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum autem venissemus Romam, permissum est Paulo manere sibimet cum custodiente se milite.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅτε δὲ ἤλθομεν εἰς ῥώμην ὁ ἑκατόνταρχος παρέδωκεν τοὺς δεσμίους τῷ στρατοπεδάρχῃ τῷ δὲ παύλῳ ἐπετράπη μένειν καθ᾽ ἑαυτὸν σὺν τῷ φυλάσσοντι αὐτὸν στρατιώτῃ
verses.king_james: And when we came to Rome, the centurion delivered the prisoners to the captain of the guard: but Paul was suffered to dwell by himself with a soldier that kept him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Post tertium autem diem convocavit primos Judæorum. Cumque convenissent, dicebat eis: Ego, viri fratres, nihil adversus plebem faciens, aut morem paternum, vinctus ab Jerosolymis traditus sum in manus Romanorum,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐγένετο δὲ μετὰ ἡμέρας τρεῖς συγκαλέσασθαι τὸν παῦλον τοὺς ὄντας τῶν ἰουδαίων πρώτους συνελθόντων δὲ αὐτῶν ἔλεγεν πρὸς αὐτούς ἄνδρες ἀδελφοί ἐγώ οὐδὲν ἐναντίον ποιήσας τῷ λαῷ ἢ τοῖς ἔθεσιν τοῖς πατρῴοις δέσμιος ἐξ ἱεροσολύμων παρεδόθην εἰς τὰς χεῖρας τῶν ῥωμαίων
verses.king_james: And it came to pass, that after three days Paul called the chief of the Jews together: and when they were come together, he said unto them, Men *and* brethren, though I have committed nothing against the people, or customs of our fathers, yet was I delivered prisoner from Jerusalem into the hands of the Romans.

verses.latin_vulgate: qui cum interrogationem de me habuissent, voluerunt me dimittere, eo quod nulla esset causa mortis in me.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἵτινες ἀνακρίναντές με ἐβούλοντο ἀπολῦσαι διὰ τὸ μηδεμίαν αἰτίαν θανάτου ὑπάρχειν ἐν ἐμοί
verses.king_james: Who, when they had examined me, would have let *me* go, because there was no cause of death in me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Contradicentibus autem Judæis, coactus sum appellare Cæsarem, non quasi gentem meam habens aliquid accusare.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀντιλεγόντων δὲ τῶν ἰουδαίων ἠναγκάσθην ἐπικαλέσασθαι καίσαρα οὐχ ὡς τοῦ ἔθνους μου ἔχων τι κατηγορῆσαι
verses.king_james: But when the Jews spake against *it*, I was constrained to appeal unto Caesar; not that I had ought to accuse my nation of.

verses.latin_vulgate: Propter hanc igitur causam rogavi vos videre, et alloqui. Propter spem enim Israël catena hac circumdatus sum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διὰ ταύτην οὖν τὴν αἰτίαν παρεκάλεσα ὑμᾶς ἰδεῖν καὶ προσλαλῆσαι ἕνεκεν γὰρ τῆς ἐλπίδος τοῦ ἰσραὴλ τὴν ἅλυσιν ταύτην περίκειμαι
verses.king_james: For this cause therefore have I called for you, to see *you*, and to speak with *you*: because that for the hope of Israel I am bound with this chain.

verses.latin_vulgate: At illi dixerunt ad eum: Nos neque litteras accepimus de te a Judæa, neque adveniens aliquis fratrum nuntiavit, aut locutus est quid de te malum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἱ δὲ πρὸς αὐτὸν εἶπον, ἡμεῖς οὔτε γράμματα περὶ σοῦ ἐδεξάμεθα ἀπὸ τῆς ἰουδαίας οὔτε παραγενόμενός τις τῶν ἀδελφῶν ἀπήγγειλεν ἢ ἐλάλησέν τι περὶ σοῦ πονηρόν
verses.king_james: And they said unto him, We neither received letters out of Judaea concerning thee, neither any of the brethren that came shewed or spake any harm of thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Rogamus autem a te audire quæ sentis: nam de secta hac notum est nobis quia ubique ei contradicitur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀξιοῦμεν δὲ παρὰ σοῦ ἀκοῦσαι ἃ φρονεῖς περὶ μὲν γὰρ τῆς αἱρέσεως ταύτης γνωστὸν ἐστιν ἡμῖν ὅτι πανταχοῦ ἀντιλέγεται
verses.king_james: But we desire to hear of thee what thou thinkest: for as concerning this sect, we know that every where it is spoken against.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum constituissent autem illi diem, venerunt ad eum in hospitium plurimi, quibus exponebat testificans regnum Dei, suadensque eis de Jesu ex lege Moysi et prophetis a mane usque ad vesperam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ταξάμενοι δὲ αὐτῷ ἡμέραν ἧκον πρὸς αὐτὸν εἰς τὴν ξενίαν πλείονες οἷς ἐξετίθετο διαμαρτυρόμενος τὴν βασιλείαν τοῦ θεοῦ πείθων τε αὐτοὺς τὰ περὶ τοῦ ἰησοῦ ἀπό τε τοῦ νόμου μωσέως καὶ τῶν προφητῶν ἀπὸ πρωῒ ἕως ἑσπέρας
verses.king_james: And when they had appointed him a day, there came many to him into *his* lodging; to whom he expounded and testified the kingdom of God, persuading them concerning Jesus, both out of the law of Moses, and *out of* the prophets, from morning till evening.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et quidam credebant his quæ dicebantur: quidam vero non credebant.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ οἱ μὲν ἐπείθοντο τοῖς λεγομένοις οἱ δὲ ἠπίστουν
verses.king_james: And some believed the things which were spoken, and some believed not.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cumque invicem non essent consentientes, discedebant, dicente Paulo unum verbum: Quia bene Spiritus Sanctus locutus est per Isaiam prophetam ad patres nostros,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀσύμφωνοι δὲ ὄντες πρὸς ἀλλήλους ἀπελύοντο εἰπόντος τοῦ παύλου ῥῆμα ἓν ὅτι καλῶς τὸ πνεῦμα τὸ ἅγιον ἐλάλησεν διὰ ἠσαΐου τοῦ προφήτου πρὸς τοὺς πατέρας ἡμῶν
verses.king_james: And when they agreed not among themselves, they departed, after that Paul had spoken one word, Well spake the Holy Ghost by Esaias the prophet unto our fathers,

verses.latin_vulgate: dicens: Vade ad populum istum, et dic ad eos: [Aure audietis, et non intelligetis, et videntes videbitis, et non perspicietis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγον πορεύθητι πρὸς τὸν λαὸν τοῦτον καὶ εἰπὲ, ἀκοῇ ἀκούσετε καὶ οὐ μὴ συνῆτε καὶ βλέποντες βλέψετε καὶ οὐ μὴ ἴδητε
verses.king_james: Saying, Go unto this people, and say, Hearing ye shall hear, and shall not understand; and seeing ye shall see, and not perceive:

verses.latin_vulgate: Incrassatum est enim cor populi hujus, et auribus graviter audierunt, et oculos suos compresserunt: ne forte videant oculis, et auribus audiant, et corde intelligant, et convertantur, et sanem eos.]
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐπαχύνθη γὰρ ἡ καρδία τοῦ λαοῦ τούτου καὶ τοῖς ὠσὶν βαρέως ἤκουσαν καὶ τοὺς ὀφθαλμοὺς αὐτῶν ἐκάμμυσαν μήποτε ἴδωσιν τοῖς ὀφθαλμοῖς καὶ τοῖς ὠσὶν ἀκούσωσιν καὶ τῇ καρδίᾳ συνῶσιν καὶ ἐπιστρέψωσιν καὶ ἰάσωμαι αὐτούς
verses.king_james: For the heart of this people is waxed gross, and their ears are dull of hearing, and their eyes have they closed; lest they should see with *their* eyes, and hear with *their* ears, and understand with *their* heart, and should be converted, and I should heal them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Notum ergo sit vobis, quoniam gentibus missum est hoc salutare Dei, et ipsi audient.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: γνωστὸν οὖν ἔστω ὑμῖν ὅτι τοῖς ἔθνεσιν ἀπεστάλη τὸ σωτήριον τοῦ θεοῦ αὐτοὶ καὶ ἀκούσονται
verses.king_james: Be it known therefore unto you, that the salvation of God is sent unto the Gentiles, and *that* they will hear it.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et cum hæc dixisset, exierunt ab eo Judæi, multam habentes inter se quæstionem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ταῦτα αὐτοῦ εἰπόντος, ἀπῆλθον οἱ ἰουδαῖοι, πολλὴν ἔχοντες ἐν ἑαυτοῖς συζήτησιν
verses.king_james: And when he had said these words, the Jews departed, and had great reasoning among themselves.

verses.latin_vulgate: Mansit autem biennio toto in suo conducto: et suscipiebat omnes qui ingrediebantur ad eum,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔμεινεν δὲ ὁ παῦλος διετίαν ὅλην ἐν ἰδίῳ μισθώματι καὶ ἀπεδέχετο πάντας τοὺς εἰσπορευομένους πρὸς αὐτόν
verses.king_james: And Paul dwelt two whole years in his own hired house, and received all that came in unto him,

verses.latin_vulgate: prædicans regnum Dei, et docens quæ sunt de Domino Jesu Christo cum omni fiducia, sine prohibitione.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: κηρύσσων τὴν βασιλείαν τοῦ θεοῦ καὶ διδάσκων τὰ περὶ τοῦ κυρίου ἰησοῦ χριστοῦ μετὰ πάσης παῤῥησίας ἀκωλύτως
verses.king_james: Preaching the kingdom of God, and teaching those things which concern the Lord Jesus Christ, with all confidence, no man forbidding him.
